title: Python Engineer - AI & LLMs
company_focus:
Data platform for energy market benchmarking, forecasting,
and valuation
description:
About Modo Energy

The energy transition is the biggest infrastructure buildout
in human history. Modo Energy is the data platform at the
centre of it.

We build the benchmarking, forecasting, and valuation tools
that the world's most serious energy investors, developers,
and operators depend on to make decisions. If a battery gets
financed, built, or traded anywhere in the world, there's a
good chance Modo data was in the room.

Founded in 2019, we're 75+ people across London, New York,
Sydney, and Madrid; $30M Series B, AI-native, and moving
fast.

This is a rare chance to join a category-defining company at
the moment it's scaling globally.

Python Engineer

At Modo Energy we’re building an AI platform for energy
markets — combining LLM agents, retrieval pipelines, and
real-time market data to create an AI analyst that can
reason about complex energy systems.

We’re an AI-native engineering team — everyone uses AI
coding tools and many of the systems we build are designed
to be consumed by AI agents as much as humans.

The role

We are hiring a Python Engineer join our Terminal team
responsible for the backend platform & infrastructure. The
Modo Terminal is our core product - a data and research
platform that energy market professionals use every day to
make better decisions. It serves real-time and historical
data, proprietary research, and analytical tools across
Global energy storage markets.

You'll own the backend systems that make the Terminal
reliable, fast, and easy to build on: APIs, databases,
deployment infrastructure, and developer tooling. As the
team grows and the product becomes more ambitious - the
platform needs to grow with it.

You'll work in a small cross-functional team with a lot of
autonomy. We value engineers who take ownership of problems
end-to-end, care about the quality of what ships, and aren't
afraid to challenge how things are done.

Responsibilities

- Build and maintain the backend services and APIs that power
  the Modo Terminal, running on Django and Python.
- Design and improve data pipelines that ingest, transform,
  and serve energy market data - ensuring reliability,
  correctness, and performance at scale.
- Own AWS infrastructure and deployment end-to-end. We use
  Terraform, and you'll be involved in the full lifecycle
  from local development to production.
- Build monitoring, observability, and alerting infrastructure
  - so we catch problems before our customers do.
- Design internal tools (CLIs, APIs, developer utilities) that
  make the team faster and reduce friction in day-to-day
  workflows.
- Work directly with domain experts (energy analysts, data
  scientists) to make sure the platform serves data that is
  accurate, timely, and useful.
- Contribute to the infrastructure that supports our LLM-
  powered AI Analyst - retrieval pipelines, tool design, and
  systems that let models work with energy market data
  effectively.

About you

- 5+ years of Python experience. Strong backend fundamentals -
  you write clean, well-structured code and you're
  comfortable owning services end-to-end, from API design
  through to deployment and monitoring.
- Real experience with AWS and infrastructure-as-code
  (Terraform). You've deployed and maintained production
  services, not just experimented in a sandbox.
- Proficiency with Django, FastAPI or similar async Python
  frameworks.
- Hands-on experience with data pipelines, relational
  databases (PostgreSQL), and building systems that serve
  data reliably at scale.
- Familiarity with LLM-powered applications is a strong plus -
  whether that's RAG pipelines, tool-calling architectures,
  or agentic systems. You don't need to be an ML engineer,
  but you should be curious and ready to build the
  infrastructure these systems run on.
- Expert-level use of AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude Code,
  GitHub Copilot, or similar). We write a lot of code with
  these tools and expect you to be fluent - knowing when to
  trust them, when to intervene, and how to get the most out
  of them.
- Good taste and judgment. You'll make constant decisions
  about system design, data modelling, and what's good
  enough to ship versus what needs more work. We want
  someone who cares about the end result, not just getting
  it to compile.
- Comfort working in a small, fast-moving team where you'll be
  expected to figure things out, make tradeoffs, and ship.
- You have full lifecycle experience, owning projects end-to-
  end from design and development, through testing into
  deployment in a live production environment.

Nice to have

- Experience with energy markets.

The Company

- At Modo Energy, we're on a mission to build the information
  architecture for the energy transition - we want to be the
  only place to come to for information on the global
  journey to net zero. Take a look at our platform, where we
  provide open access to an array of content on the energy
  transition.
- We're a dedicated and passionate team building a category-
  defining business, working on one of the world's most
  important priorities. We are looking for individuals who
  love product-building, want to work with pace at a
  mission-oriented startup, and will collaborate with us in
  shaping the culture of a rapidly growing team.
- Hybrid Work Environment: This role is hybrid, with time
  split between working from home and our London office
  (Euston Square), with in-office days from Tuesday through
  Thursday.

Salary & Benefits

- Competitive market rate – we want the best engineers!
- Employee Equity Scheme.
- Private Top-Tier Healthcare and Dental coverage with Bupa,
  Salary sacrifice Pension with 3% employer contribution; 25
  days annual leave (excluding bank holidays), plus 5
  flexible days to be taken on a Monday or a Friday. And
  lots of snacks and drinks – obviously!

What You Can Expect From Us

At Modo Energy, we believe that exceptional work deserves
exceptional reward. We're a high-performance team;
ambitious, collaborative, and genuinely motivated by the
scale of what we're trying to build.

You'll have real ownership from day one, work alongside some
of the brightest people in the industry, and be part of a
company that's defining a new category in the global energy
market. We're hybrid: everyone works Tuesday to Thursday in
office, with Monday and Friday flexible. We offer top-of-
market compensation, equity for every employee, and the
space to take your career wherever you want it to go.

We're looking for people who want to do the best work of
their careers. If that's you, we want to talk.
responsibilities: Build and maintain backend services and APIs powering the Modo Terminal using Django and Python
Design and improve data pipelines that ingest, transform, and serve energy market data at scale
Own AWS infrastructure and deployment end-to-end using Terraform from local development to production
Build monitoring, observability, and alerting infrastructure to catch problems proactively
Design internal tools (CLIs, APIs, developer utilities) to improve team productivity
Work with domain experts to ensure platform data is accurate, timely, and useful
Contribute to infrastructure supporting the LLM-powered AI Analyst including retrieval pipelines and tool design
use_cases: AI analyst that reasons about complex energy systems using LLM agents and retrieval pipelines
Energy market data platform serving real-time and historical data to investors, developers, and operators
Benchmarking, forecasting, and valuation tools for energy storage markets
Retrieval pipelines and tool-calling architectures that let LLMs work with energy market data
ai_classification_reasoning:
While the role involves contributing to infrastructure
supporting LLM-powered features (retrieval pipelines, tool
design), the primary focus is backend platform engineering —
building APIs, databases, deployment infrastructure, and
data pipelines. The AI/LLM work is a secondary
responsibility ("contribute to" rather than "own"), and the
job explicitly states "you don't need to be an ML engineer."
This is an infrastructure/platform role that enables AI
systems rather than directly building them.
